unit.note: 16 new unit/CLI tests for (phase,splitId) dedup, top-level splitId, 3-emitter --split-id pass-through (incl. is-not-None empty-string edge), and the 4 consumer de-dups (record_event, lifecycle-integrity, phase_started_emit, run single-session, compliance report, build dashboard, verifiers common, session handoff). Full affected suites green: shared/tests 3835, shared/scripts/tests 197, shared/scripts/tools/tests 145, run 335, compliance 966, plan 57.

integration.note: test_phase_completed_per_split_integration.py — real 3-split build emit->dedup->consume composition (category:integration) + single-split back-compat; integration-tests dir 184 green.

e2e.note: library monorepo — no app E2E surface

surface_verification.justification: Python plugin monorepo (scope=library); no in-tree app/web surface to drive. The tracked event-log change is exercised end-to-end by an in-process integration test (test_phase_completed_per_split_integration.py, real 3-split build) plus the full affected unit suites; the cross-repo WebUI PhaseRail consumer lives in shipwright-webui and is tracked via follow-up triage trg-941d870b.
surface_verification.runner: uv run --with pytest --with pytest-mock pytest (shared/tests + shared/scripts/{tests,tools/tests} + run + compliance + plan + integration-tests)

degraded.detail: classify_complexity flagged touches_auth from message prose; the diff touches no auth paths (src/middleware, src/lib/supabase, **/auth/**) — overridden. Authoritative diff-driven flags: cross_component (phase_session_stop hook under **/hooks/*.py) + touches_io_boundary (record_event JSON producer).

behaviors.behavior: AC1: multi-split phase records one phase_completed PER split (same phase, different splitId NOT deduped)
AC2: same (phase, splitId) is deduped (crash-resume backstop, per split)
AC2: single-split (splitId=None) back-compat — dedup by (phase, None); skip payload byte-identical; no splitId key emitted
AC2: a split-tagged end and a phase-only (None) end are distinct keys (don't collide)
AC3: --split-id promotes splitId to a top-level field on phase_started/phase_completed/phase_failed
AC3: has_phase_event keys on (phase, splitId) — matches same split, not a different split or None
AC3: explicit falsey (empty-string) splitId is forwarded on an `is not None` basis, not dropped (external-review hardening)
AC3: all 3 emitter wrappers forward --split-id from the splitId they carry (shared phase_event_emit, run single-session events, stop hook)
AC4: compliance phase-count counts DISTINCT phase names (multi-split build renders 7/7, not 9/7)
Consumer: session-handoff Recovery counts distinct phases (not the raw per-split event count)
Consumer: build-dashboard Pipeline 'Completed' shows the LATEST split's ts (not the first)
Consumer: get_latest_phase_completed_event orders by the real `ts` field (latest of N per-split ends)
Integration composition (cross_component): real 3-split build emit (shared start wrapper + stop-hook end wrapper -> real record_event CLI) -> per-split dedup -> consumer reduction
Plan SKILL step-9 emits --split-id "{split_name}" per plan split (runtime-prompt edit)
behaviors.evidence: Markdown runtime-prompt edit; the downstream per-split dedup path it exercises is covered by record_event's --split-id unit tests. Not a separately executable code behavior.

behaviors.behavior: Finalization-integrity fix: the iterate-entry reader excludes secondary-extension sidecars (<run_id>.plan.json), so retention cannot delete the real entry and find_entry_by_run_id resolves it (not the date-less plan card)
